seoTitle: Cradle Mountain vs Cradoc — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Cradle Mountain and Cradoc. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Cradle Mountain vs Cradoc: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cradle Mountain and Cradoc.
heading: Cradle Mountain vs Cradoc — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cradle Mountain, Tasmania and Cradoc, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cradle Mountain has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Cradoc (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cradle Mountain or Cradoc safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cradle Mountain has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Cradoc scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cradle Mountain scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Cradoc scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cradle Mountain or Cradoc?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cradle Mountain has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
